title: Treat empty state as semantic absence
statement: Treat emptiness first as a structural decision: remove or collapse a section with no useful next action; when the surface must remain, explain what belongs there and provide the first useful action in a compact empty state.
rationale: Design every user-visible state, but do not preserve an unnecessary container merely to render a placeholder. If absence matters, the remaining state must orient the user and support progress.

prefer: Collapse irrelevant empty containers so no artificial empty state remains.
When the surface remains, explain what belongs there and keep the first useful action compact.
avoid: Showing an empty card or zero-count section merely because the container exists.
Leaving a visible empty surface without orientation or its first useful action.

when: A proposed or existing surface has no items or content, requiring a decision whether the surface should remain visible.

evidence.summary: Asked empty sections to disappear rather than display a placeholder.
Changed external-folder emptiness to remove the folder, not show an empty container and prominent action.
Flagged an empty state that concealed a necessary keyboard shortcut.

confidence.note: Bootstrapped from independent direct user episodes under the owner's request to mine durable design rules.

lifecycle.decision_note: Activated only for the owner-requested initial bootstrap because repeated independent direct instructions or corrections establish this decision boundary.

positive_examples: Keep compact first-step guidance when it unlocks a meaningful action.
negative_examples: Showing an empty card or zero-count section merely because the container exists.
Hiding the only useful first action or shortcut.

checks: Does the empty container help the user act or understand state?
If not, can it collapse entirely?
If guidance remains, is it compact and actionable?
